title: Label Noise: The Dirty Data
concept: Label noise refers to incorrect or inconsistent labels in a dataset, which can degrade model performance.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why are 'gold standard' images mixed into labeling queues?

options.text: To continuously audit the accuracy of human annotators
To serve as background noise in training batches
To train the model on golden objects

exercises.explain: Gold standard images act as hidden tests to evaluate annotator quality and identify failing workers.

cards.title: Garbage in, Garbage out
cards.body: If you train a model with images of cats, but 10% of those images are accidentally labeled 'toaster', the model will learn that toaster elements (like metal grids and slots) belong to cats. In machine learning, dirty data corrupts the brain.

cards.title: Label Noise
cards.body: Label Noise refers to incorrect annotations in a dataset. It can be random (accidental typos or clicks) or systematic (annotators consistently mislabeling a specific class due to confusing guidelines).

cards.title: A Textbook with Typos
cards.body: Imagine learning math from a textbook where 10% of the answers in the back are completely wrong. You will get confused, doubt the correct rules you learned, and score poorly on the final exam.

cards.title: CleanLab and Data Curation
cards.body: Engineers use tools like CleanLab to detect noise. The algorithm flags images where the model is extremely confident in one class (e.g., 'Dog') but the human label says another (e.g., 'Cat'), pointing out likely typos.

cards.title: Deep learning isn't immune
cards.body: You might think modern deep neural networks can easily ignore small amounts of label noise. While they have some tolerance, noise decreases final accuracy, increases training time, and forces the model to memorize errors.

cards.title: Clean vs. Noisy Labels
cards.body: How label noise affects model decision boundaries:

left.text:
Smooth, clear decision boundary.
Model learns robust general rules.
High validation accuracy.

right.text:
Messy, jagged decision boundary.
Model overfits to memorize wrong labels.
Degraded test accuracy.

exercises.prompt: What is the definition of label noise?
exercises.explain: Label noise represents errors in labels, coordinates, or category tags in a dataset.

options.text: Incorrect or inconsistent annotations within a dataset
A high-pitched sound interference in audio recordings
The brightness variations in camera sensor pixels
The file size differences between PNG and JPEG formats

exercises.prompt: True or False: Random label noise is usually less harmful to a model's general rules than systematic, consistent label bias.
exercises.explain: True. Random noise cancels out somewhat over millions of steps, but systematic bias teaches the model wrong rules.
Overfitting happens when a model fits the noise in the training set instead of learning the underlying concepts.
exercises.sentence: If a model memorizes incorrect labels during training, it is experiencing ___.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a common diagnostic sign of high label noise in a dataset?
exercises.explain: Stalling validation accuracy despite declining training loss points to the model overfitting to labels (including incorrect ones).

options.text: The model's training loss continues to drop, but validation accuracy stalls or drops early
The image decoding speed on the GPU drops to 1 frame per second
The bounding box coordinates exceed the borders of the image file

exercises.prompt: You are building a medical diagnosis AI. You suspect that 5% of the X-rays in your massive dataset are labeled incorrectly. What is the safest course of action?

options.text: Use a curation tool to flag high-uncertainty labels, have senior doctors review those specific images, and clean them before training.
Increase the learning rate to bypass the noise.
Train the model anyway, assuming medical models ignore noise.
Delete all annotations and train the model without labels.

exercises.explain: For high-stakes medical tasks, systematic cleaning via targeted expert review is critical to prevent dangerous diagnostic errors.
